summary: This is a very brief teaser post linking to Joel Spolsky's longer article 'Seven Steps to Remarkable Customer Service.' The post uses a personal anecdote about a frustrating experience with a locksmith to hook the reader into clicking through to the full article. The squiggly-lines-of-anger imagery conveys the emotional state of a dissatisfied customer. The post serves as a promotion for the longer piece rather than containing substantive argument itself.
key_points: Uses a relatable customer frustration story as a hook for a longer article on customer service
Illustrates the emotional toll of repeated service failures from the customer's perspective
Demonstrates that even loyal customers have a breaking point after repeated poor experiences
money_quotes: I was tempted just to give up on him. But I decided to give this loser one more chance.
key_insight: Repeated customer service failures push even patient customers to the brink of abandoning a provider entirely.
